round_5_results.§11_nvlink_topology: single GPU, no NVLink (consumer card; topo X = self)
round_5_results.§11_mig_availability: NOT SUPPORTED (RTX 5070 is consumer; MIG is A100/H100 datacenter feature)

256.verdict: hexa = 0.500x cuBLAS (matches PR #214; CLOSURE row MET)

384.verdict: hexa peak 0.599x cuBLAS — BEST shape for hexa

512.verdict: hexa plateaus at ~11 TFLOPS; cuBLAS scales to 32 TFLOPS = 3.0x hexa

1024.verdict: cuBLAS 3.6x hexa at 1024; predicted §10.1 caveat CONFIRMED

round_5_results.§5m_hgemm_scale_up_interpretation: Honest negative — the §10 closure row 'HGEMM ≥ 50% cuBLAS HGEMM' is MET ONLY at M≤384 (ratio 0.500-0.599). At M=512+ the ratio drops to 0.28-0.33 (3.0-3.6x cuBLAS lead). The hexa-emit composite kernel plateaus at ~17 TFLOPS while cuBLAS scales linearly with shape. Root cause: cuBLAS at large M uses cuBLAS-LT heuristics + Hopper wgmma + larger tile geometry that hexa-emit hasn't implemented. The §10 row remains [x] for M=256 with the explicit 'single shape' caveat already documented; this round adds the measured scale-up curve confirming the caveat.
round_5_results.§6a_determinism_audit: ALL 8 PTX kernels emit ZERO atom.* + ZERO red.* (reduction) ops — DETERMINISTIC by construction. step1/2/3/5 WMMA + f16/bf16/vec_add_unroll1 + hex_neighbor
round_5_results.§11_ldmatrix_scan: 0 ldmatrix.sync.aligned ops in any WMMA kernel — hexa-emit uses wmma.load.* instead. Future codegen could use ldmatrix.sync for x4 fragment-load pipelining (sm_75+ optimization)
round_5_results.§3c_memory_hierarchy_scan: only step4_wmma_cp_async uses .shared (13 references = stage buffer + cp.async load + ld/st pairs). All other PTX uses registers + global only. .local + .const unused across the board.

§11_cuda_streams_events_smoke.dual_stream_concurrency_gain: 1.25x (sequential scheduling per concurrent_kernels=1; async_engine helps memcpy only)

round_6_results.§11_gpu_print_smoke: ptxas_rc=0 — hand-emit PTX using vprintf builtin compiles cleanly on sm_80
round_6_results.§3c_mbarrier_scan: 0 mbarrier ops in any landed PTX — sm_90+ feature unused (hexa-emit sm_80/sm_90 baseline)
round_6_results.§11_mps_daemon: NOT running (default consumer config; multi-process GPU sharing requires daemon launch)
round_6_results.§11_cupti_availability: libcupti.so.12 available at /usr/local/cuda/lib64/ + /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/ (CUPTI profiler library LINKABLE)
round_6_results.§11_mixed_arch_fat_binary: single-arch cubins (no fat-binary with multiple sm targets)
round_6_results.§11_cooperative_launch: cooperative_launch=1 · cooperative_multi_device_launch=1 (RTX 5070 supports cooperative groups API)
round_6_results.§11_persistent_kernel_cache: ~/.nv/ComputeCache: 29 files / 17 MB (JIT cache active across sessions)

round_7_results.§11_gpu_assert_smoke: ptxas_rc=0 — hand-emit PTX with __assertfail extern call compiles cleanly on sm_80
round_7_results.§11_gpu_shared_atomics_smoke: ptxas_rc=0 — atom.shared.add.s32 PTX compiles cleanly (sm_80 has atomic-on-shared support)

§11_dynamic_parallelism_caps.interpretation: unified memory + concurrent host/device access supported on RTX 5070; no display-driver watchdog (compute card mode)

round_7_results.§3c_l2_cache_modifier_hints: 0 ld.cs / ld.lu / st.cg / st.cs / st.wt / st.wb across ALL 9 PTX — hexa-emit codegen does NOT use cache-modifier hints (potential codegen improvement target)
round_7_results.ptx_inventory_summary: 29 PTX files staged on ubu-2 /tmp; 19 cubins produced; total covers cookbook 5 steps + composite HGEMM + 3 nvcc references + RFC 068 f16/bf16 vadd + RFC 069 unroll1/2 + RFC 070 hex_neighbor + gpu_print/gpu_assert/atom_shared hand-emit smokes

honest_scope: Round 5+6+7 fired ~25 codegen-side oracle + light-silicon measurements on ubu-2 RTX 5070, total wall ~6 min, $0 GPU silicon budget.

§5m HGEMM scale-up CONFIRMS the §10 'single shape' caveat: hexa peaks at M=384 (ratio 0.599), then drops to 0.280 at M=1024. §10 closure box stays [x] for M=256 (PR #214 single-shape), but operators relying on hexa-emit for large GEMM should expect 3-3.6x slower than cuBLAS at production scale.
Many PTX smokes (gpu_print, gpu_assert, atom.shared) verified ptxas accepts the hand-emit form but did NOT execute on silicon. Future cycles could exercise these via host launchers.

Most §11 brainstorm-overflow items are now either MEASURED [x] or DOCUMENTED honestly as not-applicable / not-yet-used. Remaining unchecked items are exclusively (a) codegen feature work (CSE / unroll / fusion / etc.) or (b) §10.1 blockers (multi-session compiler self-host / H100 / Mac / AMD pool).
True single-session $0 GPU.md checkbox exhaustion confirmed after Round 7. Future cycles need code (compiler self-host, new kernels) or external resources (root for Nsight, AMD pool, H100, Mac).
